title: Coelogyne microscopica
intro: Coelogyne microscopica, also known as the tiny Coelogyne orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne microscopica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Coelogyne microscopica is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. The genus Coelogyne is a group of orchids characterized by their small to medium-sized flowers and slender stems. This genus is further divided into several sections, with Coelogyne microscopica belonging to the section Coelogyne. At the species level, Coelogyne microscopica is often distinguished from other Coelogyne species by its unique flower size and coloration. Its classification within the broader context of orchid taxonomy continues to be refined by botanists and orchid enthusiasts.
